conflict: The full weight of what the First Art makes possible — Gavin can see the grammar of war, can perceive how lethal magic works at the substrate level, and understands that he could edit it — colliding with the ward failure that makes the war irrelevant
consequence: Gavin acquires the ability (or near-ability) to end the Voldemort war by rewriting the substrate rules that make lethal magic possible. His conscious decision not to do this — because the ward failure is the real emergency — is the novel's thematic crystallization
must_land_beats: Gavin thinking in the First Art rather than translating it — the shift from word-by-word translation to thinking in the new language
Watching the Cruciatus Curse at the substrate level: seeing the grammar of pain as a specific, editable agreement. Not special or powerful — just a rule
The realization that he could make lethal magic grammatically invalid. Not block it, not counter it — eliminate the substrate agreement that makes it possible

The decision not to act: quiet, undramatic, made from the perspective that the ward failure renders the war a rounding error. The decision must feel rational from Gavin's frame and monstrous from the human one
pressure_source: The full weight of what the First Art makes possible — Gavin can see the grammar of war, can perceive how lethal magic works at the substrate level, and understands that he could edit it — colliding with the ward failure that makes the war irrelevant
state_shift: Gavin acquires the ability (or near-ability) to end the Voldemort war by rewriting the substrate rules that make lethal magic possible. His conscious decision not to do this — because the ward failure is the real emergency — is the novel's thematic crystallization
